title: Ahmad Shah & The Durrani Empire
description: Explore the founding of modern Afghanistan and the historical roots of the Pashtun people.
author: Poha Editorial

content.heading: The Father of the Nation
lines: The history of modern Afghanistan is inextricably linked to the Pashtun tribes. In 1747, a young military commander named Ahmad Shah Abdali was chosen by a Loya Jirga (grand assembly) to lead the Pashtun tribes.
He took the title 'Durr-i-Durrani' (Pearl of Pearls) and founded the Durrani Empire. Today, he is affectionately known by Pashtuns as Ahmad Shah Baba — the Father of the Nation.

content.alt: The blue-domed shrine of Ahmad Shah Durrani in Kandahar
content.caption: The tomb of Ahmad Shah Durrani in Kandahar, the first capital of the Durrani Empire.

content.text: At its peak in the late 18th century, the Durrani Empire was one of the largest Islamic empires in the world, stretching from eastern Iran to northern India (including modern-day Pakistan and Kashmir).

content.heading: The Loya Jirga Tradition
lines: Ahmad Shah's rise to power highlights a core Pashtun institution: the Jirga. A Jirga is a traditional assembly of tribal elders that makes decisions by consensus.
The 'Loya Jirga' (Grand Council) remains a vital political mechanism in the region today, invoked during times of national crisis or to ratify constitutions.

content.question: What is the traditional Pashtun assembly of elders used for decision-making called?
content.explanation: A Jirga is a traditional assembly of leaders that makes decisions by consensus according to Pashtunwali. A 'Loya Jirga' is a grand, national-level assembly.
